sources.use: Transient user activation is browser-maintained state and must be sampled at gesture time; it does not prove audible output.
AudioContext runtime state and routing describe browser audio processing prerequisites, not human audibility.

finding: A physical-device audio report needs a single bounded capture session that records transient activation synchronously, then runtime state, then the human outcome in temporal order. Free-form notes, analyser arrays, and unrelated device data are not required and should be excluded.
design_implication: Use createAudioCaptureSession as the privacy-minimal adapter before classification and deployment binding. Require physical_iphone, active transient activation, complete running audio route, ordered timestamps, opaque session identity, exact commit and immutable deployment identity, and a ten-minute maximum session duration.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files

limits: The adapter does not itself prove that sound was heard.
The physical iPhone and immutable deployment assertions still require external verification.
The adapter is not yet wired into the deployed Vercel UI.
No cryptographic signature is produced by this module.
falsification_route: Reject the design if it permits a runtime or human timestamp before activation.
Reject the design if extra free-form or identifying fields survive output allowlisting.
Revise the duration bound if real physical verification cannot reliably complete within ten minutes.
Reject any downstream claim of audibility that lacks an explicit heard outcome from the same session.
privacy_boundary: Stores only exact build/deployment identifiers, opaque session ID, coarse physical-device class, activation booleans, minimal runtime booleans, ordered timestamps, and a binary heard/not_heard outcome.
